title: Images built with docker-compose (via `podman compose`) are assigned to `docker.io/library` registry
body:
### Issue Description

When building an image with docker-compose, its name is prefixed with the `docker.io/library` registry name instead of `localhost`.

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. Install `docker-compose`, so that it is usable by `podman compose`
2. Create a `Dockerfile` file with content:
```Dockerfile
FROM scratch
```
3. Create a compose file `compose.yaml` in the same directory with content:
```yaml
name: test-project
services:
  test-service:
    build: .
```
4. Run `podman compose build` in this directory


### Describe the results you received

A new image was created with name `docker.io/library/test-project-test-service`:

```
Sending build context to Docker daemon     372B
STEP 1/2: FROM scratch
STEP 2/2: LABEL "com.docker.compose.image.builder"="classic"
COMMIT docker.io/library/test-project-test-service
--> 635e9c6bae57
Successfully tagged docker.io/library/test-project-test-service:latest
635e9c6bae576c4cda064f8ed705a55762cbc0e0e28432c5980a33de5d56db56
Successfully built 635e9c6bae57
Successfully tagged test-project-test-service
```

### Describe the results you expected

The created image should use the name `localhost/test-project-test-service`.

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

Rootless

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

Docker Compose version v2.30.1

### Additional information

comments.body: I *think* this is an assumption made by docker compose code itself ?  I would need to verify that but I have a vague recollection about this.

I am pretty sure this is enforced by the compat API to match docker behavior.

You can set compat_api_enforce_docker_hub=false in containers.conf to disable this AFAIK.
https://github.com/containers/common/blob/main/docs/containers.conf.5.md
